issue.title: Borrow or adapt Haxe PHP runtime/std behavior

artifacts.role: deterministic stock Haxe PHP runtime/std strategy probe runner
accepted WPHX PHP runtime/std strategy ADR
runtime/std strategy manifest with stock-target executable probe observations
compiler operations documentation updated with runtime/std strategy lane

claims: Stock Haxe PHP remains the runtime/std behavior oracle and borrowing source for WPHX PHP until a later backend-promotion ADR moves that ownership.
The executable probe records stock boot shape, arrays/maps/iterators, closures, exceptions, StringTools/JSON behavior, and debug source-map/source-comment behavior.
WPHX PHP public files that do not need stock implementation/runtime behavior should avoid stock Haxe bootstrap; delegated implementation shells remain governed by ADR-014.
non_claims: This records stock Haxe PHP runtime/std oracle behavior; it does not claim WPHX PHP owns that behavior.
This does not claim stock Haxe PHP public output is acceptable for WordPress distribution files.
This does not remove stock Haxe PHP private implementation fallbacks.
This does not prove full reflection, dynamic dispatch, Unicode/string, iterator, or exception coverage.
